Home / Services / Historic Bath Preservation

Historic Bath Preservation

Restoring original charm while adding modern functionality to Boston's vintage bathrooms.

Boston's historic homes often feature irreplaceable architectural details that deserve careful preservation. Our team specializes in restoring original clawfoot tubs, hex tile flooring, and vintage fixtures while discreetly updating plumbing and electrical systems.

We carefully document existing conditions before work begins, using reversible installation methods whenever possible. For Back Bay brownstones and South End townhomes, we source reproduction moldings and period-appropriate hardware to maintain architectural integrity. Modern comforts like discreet radiant heating and low-flow plumbing are integrated without compromising historic character.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Historic assessment and documentation of existing features
  2. 02 Step 2: Custom solution planning with preservation specialists
  3. 03 Step 3: Gentle demolition protecting salvageable elements
  4. 04 Step 4: Installation with reversible techniques and period materials

Benefits

  • Preserves your home's architectural value
  • Meets local historic district requirements
  • Modern functionality hidden in vintage aesthetics
  • Specialized craftsmen trained in restoration techniques

Frequently Asked Questions

Can you match my original 1920s subway tile?

Yes, we work with local artisans who recreate historic tile patterns and glaze colors. For extensive projects, we can source original salvaged materials from Boston architectural reclaimers.

How do you handle lead paint in old bathrooms?

Our team is EPA-certified in lead-safe practices. We contain the work area with plastic barriers and use HEPA vacuums, following all Massachusetts lead abatement protocols.

Will a historic remodel take longer than new construction?

Typically adding 1-2 weeks for careful demolition and custom fabrication. We build this into our timeline upfront to avoid surprises.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with historic bath preservation? Contact us today.
